sections.content:
Now I see the board changed! The bottom-right grid - the bottom-right grid is now has all red squares instead of blue! The grid changed from all blue to all red. Let me check what changed.
Looking at the image, the bottom-right grid (which had a dark gray background) now shows all red squares. The bottom-right grid was blue, now all red.
This suggests clicking on blue squares in the bottom-right grid toggles them to red. Let me verify this.
Let me check what the board looks like now and understand the pattern.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
# Check bottom-right grid colors now
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['b', 'R'] in ['b', 'R'] and n['pixels'] == 36:
        b = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
        if min_r >= 36:
            print(n['id'], n['color'], min_r, min_c)
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game-tier fields: initial state, no prior knowledge yet", "level-tier fields: initial state, no prior knowledge yet"}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
# Try clicking on a red square in bottom-right grid to toggle it back

    if n['color'] == 'R' and n['pixels'] == 36:

            action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': min_r, 'col': min_c}])
